page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server is designed without local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and external resources. It is highly optimized for network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This high-throughput networking capability makes it suitable for data-intensive workloads that require rapid communication across distributed nodes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based arm64 architecture provides a high density of physical cores and high network throughput relative to its resource profile, offering a favorable performance-per-watt ratio for scale-out workloads. The instance is well-suited for high-performance computing (HPC), network appliances, distributed analytics, and large-scale containerized microservices that benefit from high core counts and high-speed networking.

It features no local storage or GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, optimizing it for network and EBS-heavy operations. The high core density and massive network throughput make this bare-metal instance highly efficient for distributed computing, high-performance network appliances, and large-scale containerized workloads.
